title: Harbor Crest Realty website visual refresh: license one large Adobe Stock architecture hero, brand-grade it, crop/reframe into desktop + tablet + mobile heroes, section headers and banner ads, and vectorize the logo for placement
grounding_note: VERBATIM from the Freelancer.com "Vibrant Social & Web Graphics" brief: the client wants "matching website graphics—hero images, section headers, and call-to-action spots—plus occasional banner ads for promotional campaigns"; every asset should "feel part of one cohesive family while being perfectly sized, compressed, and formatted for its destination"; deliverables include "Web-ready exports in the correct dimensions and file types" and a "Mini style reference (colours, typography) to keep future assets consistent." REASONABLY ENRICHED to make it a runnable, GEN-FREE Adobe-only benchmark and to fit the real_estate_property vertical: the generic brand is replaced with a fictional boutique real-estate brokerage, Harbor Crest Realty, and its persona (marketing lead Priya Deshmukh). The brief names hero images and correct-dimension web exports but does not name a visual source; per the slot's O8 stock-hero rule and its replacement note ("license a correctly-sized stock image + crop/reframe + place logo, NOT expand a small image"), the hero originates as a LICENSED Adobe Stock architecture photograph shot at large resolution (asset_search -> asset_license_and_download_stock), then is brand-graded and CROPPED/REFRAMED into each required web slot — it is NOT expanded/outpainted from a small image (no generative expand/outpaint, no generative fill, no upscale). The concrete brand palette (deep harbor navy #17324B, warm sand #C9A66B accent, off-white #F5F1E8) and the ~2560px hero band + tablet/mobile/section-header/banner dimensions are direct realizations of "perfectly sized... for its destination" and "correct dimensions." The supplied flat logo raster is vectorized (the brief's "typography/style reference" + reusable cohesive family). No from-scratch illustration or generative art is claimed — every visual is a crop/reframe/tonal-grade of one licensed photo or a client raster.
one_line_ask: License one large Adobe Stock architecture photograph, brand-grade it to the Harbor Crest palette, then crop/reframe that single graded master into a desktop hero, tablet hero, mobile hero, two section-header strips, and two promo banner sizes, vectorize the client logo, and deliver the cohesive web-ready set with a font recommendation to a shared Creative Cloud folder.
full_brief: Harbor Crest Realty is a boutique waterfront-and-city brokerage whose website structure and copy already work, but the imagery feels dated and generic and no longer signals the premium, trustworthy feel we want. Refresh the visual layer into ONE cohesive, web-ready graphics family, all cut from a single hero look so the site feels intentional. (1) HERO SOURCE: source a high-quality, aspirational real-estate architecture photograph from Adobe Stock — a modern light-filled open living space or a clean contemporary home exterior at golden hour, no recognizable faces, no readable signage or brand marks — and license it at LARGE resolution (we need it big enough to cut multiple crops from, roughly 5000px on the long edge; do NOT stretch or AI-expand a small file). (2) BRAND GRADE: color-grade the licensed photo to our palette — primary deep harbor navy #17324B, warm sand accent #C9A66B, off-white #F5F1E8: auto-tone for a clean baseline, warm the white balance slightly toward the golden-hour sand tone, lift the shadows so interiors read open and airy, recover highlights so windows keep detail, and push vibrance just enough that the scene looks premium without being over-cooked. This graded file is the single MASTER every web slot is cut from. (3) RESPONSIVE HEROES (crop/reframe only, no expansion): from the graded master, produce a desktop hero 2560x1080 (~21:9 band), a tablet hero 1536x1024, and a mobile hero 1080x1350 (4:5 portrait) — each framed so the focal architecture survives the reframe. (4) SECTION HEADERS: cut two low-band section-header strips 1920x480 from the master for the 'Featured Listings' and 'About Us' sections. (5) PROMO BANNERS: cut two banner-ad sizes for our seasonal open-house campaign — a leaderboard 1200x628 and a square 1080x1080 — and give the square banner a subtle navy monochromatic treatment so it reads as the stylized promo variant rather than a plain photo. (6) LOGO: we'll supply our flat wordmark as a PNG; knock the background to transparency and vectorize it into a clean SVG so it stays razor-sharp placed over any of these graphics at any size. (7) HAND-OFF: recommend an on-brand heading/label typeface pairing for the mini style reference, and upload the finished web-ready set back to a shared Creative Cloud folder so the whole cohesive family lands in one place for my team. Deliverables: brand-graded master, 3 responsive hero crops, 2 section-header strips, 2 promo banners (leaderboard + square navy variant), 1 transparent logo SVG, a font-pairing note, and a shared CC folder. Everything must be perfectly sized to the dimensions above and feel part of one family.

inputs.name: harbor_stock_search_intent.json
inputs.gen_prompt: Output a short JSON object describing the Adobe Stock search a real-estate marketing lead would run for a website hero, e.g. {"query":"modern light-filled open-plan living room with floor-to-ceiling windows at golden hour, contemporary architecture, warm wood and stone, no people","orientation":"landscape","min_resolution":"5000x3333","license":"standard","mood":"premium, aspirational, warm, trustworthy","exclusions":"no faces, no visible brand signage or logos, no text overlays"}. This is the client's creative direction expressed as a stock query and drives asset_search/license.
inputs.realism_notes: Represents Harbor Crest's creative direction, not a pixel asset; the min_resolution ~5000px is deliberately large so the chain can CROP multiple web slots from one master rather than expand a small file. Realistic agency input.

inputs.name: harbor_stock_hero_source.jpg
inputs.gen_prompt: Photoreal wide-angle architectural interior photograph, ~5000x3333 landscape, of a modern light-filled open-plan living room with floor-to-ceiling windows overlooking a calm harbor at golden hour: warm oak floors, pale stone feature wall, minimalist furniture, soft raking sunlight and long natural shadows, blurred water and distant coastline beyond the glass, no people, no readable signage or brand marks anywhere, real glass reflections and true depth-of-field falloff, natural sensor grain, subtle lens vignette, candid real-estate-listing realism, no CGI gloss, no watermark. This stands in for the licensable large-resolution Adobe Stock hero the asset_search/license step returns.
inputs.realism_notes: Must look like a genuine licensable stock photo, not CGI: real reflections, natural grain, no watermark, no faces/logos. Large landscape source (~5000px) so the chain can crop desktop/tablet/mobile/header/banner slots WITHOUT any generative expand or upscale.

inputs.name: harbor_crest_wordmark.png
inputs.gen_prompt: Clean flat vector-style real-estate wordmark on a plain flat light-gray (#EEEEEE) background reading 'HARBOR CREST REALTY' in a confident refined serif with a small simple line-drawn lighthouse/cresting-wave mark to the left, single deep navy color #17324B, crisp high-contrast edges, no gradient, no shadow, no photographic texture, ~1600x600px — the kind of flat logo raster a client hands a designer to be vectorized.
inputs.realism_notes: Client-supplied brand mark; flat high-contrast single-color art on a uniform removable background so remove_background + vectorize produce a clean small SVG. Text/logo asset -> gemini-3-pro-image.

inputs.name: harbor_brand_kit.json
inputs.gen_prompt: Output a small JSON brand sheet for the fictional brokerage: {"brand":"Harbor Crest Realty","contact":"Priya Deshmukh, Marketing Lead","navy":"#17324B","sand":"#C9A66B","offwhite":"#F5F1E8","voice":"premium, calm, trustworthy, coastal","web_slots":{"desktop_hero":"2560x1080","tablet_hero":"1536x1024","mobile_hero":"1080x1350","section_header":"1920x480","banner_leaderboard":"1200x628","banner_square":"1080x1080"},"note":"one cohesive family cut from a single graded hero master; correct dimensions per destination"}. These are the brand colors, contact persona, and required web dimensions the brief implies.
inputs.realism_notes: The 'mini style reference' the brief names, expressed as a brand sheet; drives the grade hex, crop dimensions, and monochromatic navy hue for the square banner. IP-clean fictional brand + persona.

outputs.name: hero_master_graded.jpg
outputs.spec: The brand-graded full-resolution master (~5000px landscape) — auto-toned, warmed toward the sand tone, shadows lifted, highlights recovered, vibrance pushed to the Harbor Crest look. Single source every web slot is cropped from.

outputs.name: hero_desktop_2560x1080.png
outputs.spec: Desktop hero band, 2560x1080 (~21:9) PNG, cropped/reframed from the graded master with the focal architecture preserved. Web-ready.

outputs.name: hero_tablet_1536x1024.png
outputs.spec: Tablet hero, 1536x1024 PNG, cropped/reframed from the same graded master.

outputs.name: hero_mobile_1080x1350.png
outputs.spec: Mobile portrait hero, 1080x1350 (4:5) PNG, cropped/reframed from the graded master, focal area preserved.

outputs.name: section_header_featured_1920x480.png
outputs.spec: 'Featured Listings' section-header strip, 1920x480 PNG, low band cropped from the graded master.

outputs.name: section_header_about_1920x480.png
outputs.spec: 'About Us' section-header strip, 1920x480 PNG, a different low band cropped from the graded master so the two headers vary but stay in-family.

outputs.name: banner_openhouse_leaderboard_1200x628.png
outputs.spec: Open-house promo leaderboard banner, 1200x628 PNG, cropped/reframed from the graded master.

outputs.name: banner_openhouse_square_navy.png
outputs.spec: Open-house promo square banner, 1080x1080 PNG, cropped from the master then given a subtle navy (#17324B) monochromatic treatment so it reads as the stylized branded promo variant.

outputs.name: harbor_crest_logo.svg
outputs.spec: Transparent-background vectorized Harbor Crest wordmark, clean scalable SVG for crisp placement over any hero/banner at any size.

outputs.name: font_pairing_recommendation.txt
outputs.spec: Recommended heading/label typeface pairing matching the premium coastal look, from font_recommend, for the mini style reference.

connector_workflow.note: Search Adobe Stock for a modern, aspirational real-estate architecture hero (light-filled interior / golden-hour exterior, no faces, no signage) using the client's creative direction; returns low-res candidate previews + asset IDs. Filter to large-resolution results so crops can be cut without expansion.

License the chosen candidate BEFORE editing and download the full-resolution (~5000px) presigned file. This large licensed photo is the single seed the rest of the chain grades and crops. (In execution this resolves to harbor_stock_hero_source.jpg.)
Neutral auto-tone baseline on the licensed photo before the manual brand grade.
Warm the white balance slightly toward the golden-hour sand tone (#C9A66B accent direction) so the scene reads premium and inviting.
Lift the shadows/dark portions so the interior reads open and airy rather than heavy.
Recover the bright window/sunlit highlights so the glass keeps detail in the crops.
Push vibrance just enough for a premium look without over-cooking — completes the GRADED MASTER that every web slot is cropped from.
Crop/reframe the graded master to the 2560x1080 desktop hero band, keeping the focal architecture. No expansion — the master is large enough to crop directly.
Crop/reframe the graded master to the 1536x1024 tablet hero.
Crop/reframe the graded master to the 1080x1350 (4:5) mobile portrait hero, focal area preserved.
Cut a 1920x480 low-band strip from the graded master for the 'Featured Listings' section header.
Cut a different 1920x480 low band from the master for the 'About Us' section header so the two headers vary while staying in-family.
Crop/reframe the master to the 1200x628 open-house leaderboard promo banner.
Crop the master to a 1080x1080 square base for the open-house square promo banner.
Lay a subtle navy #17324B monochromatic treatment over the square base so it reads as the stylized branded promo variant rather than a plain photo (deliverable).
Knock the flat gray background of the client wordmark out to transparency before vectorizing (-> transparent PNG only, no compositing).
Vectorize the transparent wordmark into a clean, infinitely-scalable single-color SVG for crisp placement over any hero/banner.
Recommend an on-brand premium/coastal heading + label typeface pairing (existing typefaces only) for the mini style reference, driven by the brand kit voice and palette.
Create the shared CC delivery folder that will hold the full web-ready family for Priya's team.
Open an upload session into the delivery folder to push the finished deliverables back to shared CC storage.

connector_workflow.inputs_from: upload_session, hero_tablet_1536x1024.png, hero_mobile_1080x1350.png, section_header_featured_1920x480.png, section_header_about_1920x480.png, banner_openhouse_leaderboard_1200x628.png, banner_openhouse_square_navy.png, harbor_crest_logo.svg
connector_workflow.note: Finalize the upload of the master, all hero crops, both section headers, both banners and the logo SVG to the CC delivery folder; returns the asset URNs and completes the shared-folder hand-off for Priya's team.

chaining_note: Strict output->input chain with no orphan steps; the deliverable family emerges entirely from one licensed seed photo plus the client logo raster. step1 asset_search(harbor_stock_search_intent.json)->stock_candidates -> step2 license_and_download_stock(stock_candidates)->hero_licensed_fullres.jpg (single large seed). Grade sub-chain in series, each consuming the prior output: step3 auto_tone->hero_autotoned -> step4 color_temperature->hero_warmed -> step5 dark_portions->hero_shadows_lifted -> step6 highlights->hero_highlights_recovered -> step7 vibrance->hero_master_graded.jpg (the MASTER). Seven web slots branch off that single master via crop/reframe only: step8 desktop 2560x1080, step9 tablet 1536x1024, step10 mobile 1080x1350, step11 featured section header 1920x480, step12 about section header 1920x480, step13 leaderboard banner 1200x628, and step14 square base 1080 -> step15 monochromatic_tint->banner_openhouse_square_navy.png (the stylized promo variant). Logo branch: step16 remove_background(harbor_crest_wordmark.png)->wordmark_knockout.png -> step17 vectorize->harbor_crest_logo.svg. step18 font_recommend consumes the brand kit for the style-reference type pairing. Packaging consumes the finals: step19 create_folders->delivery_folder; step20 init_upload(folder + master + desktop hero)->upload_session; step21 finalize_upload(session + the remaining crops/headers/banners/logo)->cc_assets_urns (the finished set delivered to shared CC storage). Every non-initial step consumes a named prior output; the three heroes, two headers, two banners and logo SVG are all downstream products of the licensed hero or client raster.
difficulty_rationale: Long-horizon and wide-mix: 21 connector calls, all server="adobe", spanning 15 DISTINCT Adobe tools across three capability groups (asset/stock/storage/board, raster photo-edit, and vector). It is a genuine multi-stage grade-and-reframe pipeline, not a one-shot edit: stock sourcing + licensing at large resolution -> a five-step brand tonal grade (auto-tone -> color-temperature -> dark-portions -> highlights -> vibrance) producing a single master -> SEVEN cohesive web slots cropped/reframed from that one master at exact required dimensions -> a navy monochromatic promo variant -> logo knockout + vectorize -> font recommendation -> CC folder upload hand-off. Hard parts that test the agent: (a) ordering correctly (license before edit; auto-tone before manual adjustments); (b) treating the crops as REFRAMES of one graded master so the whole set reads as one family at correct destination dimensions; (c) confining the promo treatment to the square variant only; (d) reusing one licensed hero across seven output geometries. This is the GEN-FREE O8 stock-hero pattern the slot's replacement_note prescribes: the hero is a LICENSED large stock photo that is CROPPED to each slot, NOT expanded from a small file — generative expand/outpaint is deliberately NOT used. Every step is [C] headless-confirmed. It avoids all banned capabilities: no generative expand/outpaint, no generative fill, no text-to-image / from-scratch art or logo, no object-removal-with-reconstruction, no upscaling/super-resolution, no bespoke letterform design, no layout composition (no render_layout/render_vector, no Express [W] widget track, no Canva). image_fill_area is not used. image_remove_background is used legitimately for the logo knockout (-> transparent PNG), not compositing.

reverify.banned_capability_check: Confirmed NONE of the banned capabilities are used: no generative expand/outpaint / reframe-expand (this is the GEN-FREE replacement per the slot's replacement_note — the large stock hero is licensed at ~5000px and CROPPED to each slot, never expanded); no generative fill of new content; no text-to-image or from-scratch art/logo (the hero is licensed Adobe Stock, the logo is a client-supplied raster that is only knocked-out + vectorized); no upscaling / super-resolution (crops go from a large master down to smaller web sizes only); no object-removal-with-reconstruction / inpainting; no bespoke font/letterform design (font_recommend only suggests existing typefaces); no unreachable external account. image_fill_area (solid-fill) is not used.
reverify.notes: Corrected from the authored draft: category renamed stock_hero_expand -> stock_hero_reframe because the token 'expand' names the exact banned capability this task deliberately avoids and could trip a keyword filter; nothing else about the slot-match changed (still O8 / Photo & Image / image / create / real_estate_property). Each potentially-risky step stays in-bounds: (1) the seven image_crop_and_resize steps (n8-14) are plain geometry crops from a genuinely large licensed master, which is exactly why the source min_resolution is ~5000px and why NO expansion is needed — the category's expand risk is designed out. (2) asset_license_and_download_stock (n2) licenses BEFORE any edit. (3) image_apply_monochromatic_tint (n15) is a valid single-hue brand-color tonal treatment on the square banner, in-palette, not compositing. (4) image_remove_background (n16) -> transparent PNG only, feeding image_vectorize (n17) for a clean logo SVG — no compositing or occluded-pixel reconstruction. (5) font_recommend (n18) recommends existing typefaces from the brand kit, no letterform design. (6) asset_create_folders / asset_initialize_file_upload / asset_finalize_file_upload (n19-21) deliver the finished web-ready family into a shared CC folder — the honest hand-off surface, no review-board deep-link needed. All 21 steps are server='adobe', [C] headless (n2 stock license included), no [W]/[A]/[T]/[X] steps; tool_call_count=21 equals the number of connector_workflow steps; distinct_adobe_tools=15 equals the distinct tools in tools_used and in the workflow; 15 >= 6 and 21 >= 10 — qualifies as a long-horizon flagship.
